title: Prime Minister

result_summary.note: Active + embarked only. Reserves are not observable.

final_assessment:
## Military Posture Assessment - Q1 2026

**Current Force Disposition:**
Levantia maintains a concentrated defensive posture at hex (6,10) with 9 active units forward-deployed. Our forces include three ground units (two on the Lebanon northern front, one facing Gaza), all three tactical air squadrons fully committed (F-35I, F-15I, F-16I), two active air defense systems (Iron Dome northern, David's Sling central), and one strategic missile unit. Seven units remain in reserve: three ground (West Bank, northern home defense, southern Persia-facing), one tactical air (UAV/drone wing), one air defense (Arrow southern), and two strategic missile units.

**Strategic Context:**
We are actively at war with Persia, which has deployed 15 observable units across three global hexes (6,12), (7,13), and (8,13). Persian forces significantly outnumber our active deployment - they field 8 ground units, 6 tactical air, and 1 air defense system. Our treasury is healthy at 10.0 with moderate war tiredness (2.0) and stable governance.

**Key Vulnerabilities & Strengths:**
Our main vulnerability is force concentration - nearly all active units are clustered in one hex, creating a single point of failure. However, this maximizes defensive coordination and air defense coverage. Persia's superior numbers (15 vs 9 active) present a significant threat, but our technological edge (F-35I stealth capability) and layered air defenses provide qualitative advantages. Our strategic missile reserves offer deterrent capability, while Persian forces appear dispersed across Iranian territory, potentially reducing their concentrated striking power.
